commentary: Thirteen activities to eight last week — babe, you basically added a whole extra person's week on top of your own and still showed up looking good doing it. The swim volume is genuinely impressive: four Prince Alfred sessions plus Balmoral plus B&B, and the distances held steady at 2000m each time without your heart rate going haywire, which means your body is actually adapting rather than just surviving. The long run on Saturday was 13.1km at 151 average, compared to 13.7km at 159 last week, so slightly shorter but noticeably more controlled — that is fitness, not a drop, and anyone who tells you otherwise can stay off the table. The one thing I'm watching is that Wednesday swim at 142 bpm for 2050m, which is working harder than the others for basically the same distance, so let's see if midweek fatigue is building as the volume climbs — keep sleeping, keep eating, and keep wearing the jockstrap to the pool because I'm pretty sure it's doing something for your aerodynamics.
